data.text: Using the `ddx` workflow to validate the bead contract, inspect the referenced workspace-resolution code, and then run the targeted Go tests before committing once.

docs/bead-backends.md:23:The bd/br path is wired today through `cli/internal/bead/store.go:88-137` and `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go:10-149`.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-f7f09b6e/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"erik","body":"Bead: ddx-f7f09b6e — Add beads-archive collection with transparent read-through (ADR-004 step 2)

Result: no changes needed; AC already satisfied by prior commits.

Audit against acceptance criteria
---------------------------------

AC1. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl is recognized as a valid bead collection.

  - cli/internal/bead/archive.go:11 declares
    BeadsArchiveCollection = "beads-archive".
  - cli/internal/bead/registry.go:126-130 registers the spec in the default
    registry with JSONLFile "beads-archive.jsonl" and LockDirName
    "beads-archive.lock".
  - The archive partner store opens via NewStoreWithCollection(s.Dir,
    BeadsArchiveCollection) (archive.go:42), so the JSONL backend resolves
    it to .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl by the same registry path.

AC2. ddx bead show <id> finds beads in either active or archive.

  - cli/cmd/bead.go:319 wires `bead show` to s.GetWithArchive.
  - Implementation: archive.go:164-177 — first tries the active Get, then
    falls back to the archivePartner. Active-collection guard at line 168
    prevents recursion when the caller is already on the archive collection.
  - Test: TestArchiveGetWithArchiveFallsBack
    (cli/internal/bead/archive_test.go:150-183) seeds an active and an
    archived bead, runs Archive(), and asserts GetWithArchive resolves both
    IDs and errors on a missing one. PASS.

AC3. ddx bead list has a --include-archive flag (or equivalent) and the
default behavior is documented.

  - cli/cmd/bead.go:846 declares the equivalent flag:
    cmd.Flags().Bool("all", false, "Include archived beads
    (.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl)").
  - Default behavior is to consult the archive partner via ListWithArchive
    (bead.go:808-812). The flag is a no-op kept for compatibility, with the
    behavior documented in the inline comment at bead.go:808-810.
  - Implementation: archive.go:182-209 — union with active-wins precedence,
    archive-missing tolerated as empty.
  - Test: TestArchiveListWithArchiveIncludesBoth (archive_test.go:216-245)
    exercises both the active-only List() and the merged ListWithArchive().

AC4. ddx bead ready / blocked still operate only on the active collection.

  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1224-1344 — Ready() / ReadyExecution() route
    through readyFiltered() which calls s.ReadAll(), the active-collection
    read. The archive partner is never consulted.
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1347-1469 — Blocked() / BlockedAll() also
    call s.ReadAll(); no archive read-through.
  - Test: TestArchiveReadyAndBlockedQueryActiveOnly
    (archive_test.go:185-214) seeds a closed-and-archived bead alongside an
    open active bead, runs Archive() so the closed entry truly leaves the
    active file, then asserts Ready() returns only the open bead and
    Blocked() is empty. PASS.

AC5. ddx work / agent execute-loop ignores archive when picking next work.

  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:199 declares the worker's Store
    interface as ReadyExecution()-only.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1088 — w.Store.ReadyExecution()
    is the queue source, which (per AC4) runs against the active collection
    only.
  - The same readyFiltered active-only ReadAll covers this path; no
    additional plumbing reaches into the archive partner from the loop.

AC6. New tests cover read-through for show, and active-only behavior for
ready/blocked.

  - Read-through for show: TestArchiveGetWithArchiveFallsBack
    (archive_test.go:150).
  - Active-only behavior for ready/blocked:
    TestArchiveReadyAndBlockedQueryActiveOnly (archive_test.go:185).
  - Supporting coverage: TestArchiveMovesEligibleClosedBeads (line 36),
    TestArchivePreservesReferencedClosedBeads (line 119),
    TestArchiveListWithArchiveIncludesBoth (line 216),
    TestArchiveOpportunisticTriggerOnClose (line 247).

Verification
------------

  cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...   → ok (4.933s)
  cd cli && go test ./cmd/ -run TestBead  → ok (1.617s)

Why nothing was committed
-------------------------

The bead description treats archive read-through as net-new work building on
ddx-2f453147. The earlier slice in this ADR-004 decomposition already shipped
the registry, the archive partner store, GetWithArchive / ListWithArchive,
the `bead show` and `bead list` wiring in cli/cmd/bead.go, the active-only
behavior of Ready/Blocked/ReadyExecution (which falls out of using ReadAll
on the active store), and the matching tests in archive_test.go. There is
no remaining gap to fill for any of the six AC items, so committing
additional code or tests would be churn rather than progress.

If a future review disagrees and identifies a specific AC item that is in
fact unmet, the natural next slice would be to add the missing piece (e.g.
a fully-isolated `--include-archive` flag distinct from the legacy `--all`
no-op, or a dedicated cmd-level integration test for `bead show` against an
archived ID). None of those gaps are visible in the current code.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-ee2cd434/events.jsonl:12:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes_rationale — bead ddx-ee2cd434 (SD-024 Stage 2)

(a) WHAT IS DONE
----------------
The previous attempt (commit 5ae02964) migrated the high-value `r.Run(RunOptions{...})`
and `RunViaService(..., RunOptions{...})` test sites onto the SD-024 successor API
(`r.RunWithConfig` + `RunWithConfigViaService` + `AgentRunRuntime`) across:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go    (~22 sites: TestRunWith*, TestSessionLogging,
                                         TestRunPromptFile, TestRunStdinMode,
                                         TestRunModel*, TestRunNonZeroExit,
                                         TestRunUnknownHarness, TestRunEmptyPrompt,
                                         TestIntegration_CodexEcho, TestRunOpencode*,
                                         and the *Permissions* pair)
  - cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go   (lmstudio / openrouter dispatch regressions)
  - cli/internal/agent/script_test.go   (runScript helper now builds rcfg via
                                         TestRunConfigOpts and dispatches via
                                         RunWithConfigViaService)

Those 26 sites are the bulk of the "test sites that exercise the high-level agent
run path" called out in the bead description.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING
--------------------
The bead AC reads:

    rg 'RunOptions\{|CompareOptions\{|QuorumOptions\{' cli/ returns 0 matches outside
    the type definitions about to be retired.

This is a *global* gate. It cannot be satisfied by test-only changes — it requires
retiring every remaining `RunOptions{...}` construction in both production and test
code. The remaining sites fall into two classes:

CLASS A — tests that call functions whose signature *takes* RunOptions, so the
RunOptions construction is not a "test site" the test could rewrite without
changing the function under test:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go        BuildArgs(h, RunOptions{...}, model)  — 14 sites
                                            BuildArgs is the function under test.
  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go
        line 78  resolvePrompt(RunOptions{PromptFile})              — exercises r.resolvePrompt
        line 85  defaultResolvePromptForCompare(RunOptions{...})    — exercises pkg-level helper
        line 112 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})  — exercises stub-injection helper
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go
        line 195 SessionIndexEntryFromResult(root, RunOptions{...}, ...)
        line 278 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/models_test.go
        line 45  BenchmarkArmsToCompare(arms, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/virtual_test.go
        lines 164/174/214 runVirtualFn(runner, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go
        lines 206/212/218/224/291/324  internal opts: RunOptions{...} fixtures

Migrating any of these test sites requires first changing the *signature* of the
production function under test (BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare,
RunViaServiceWith, runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, SessionIndexEntryFromResult,
plus the internal claude_stream helpers). That is production-code refactor work,
explicitly outside the scope the bead description names.

CLASS B — production callers still constructing RunOptions{...}:

  - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go              :1462
  - cli/internal/server/server.go     :1913, :3786
  - cli/internal/exec/store.go        :201
  - cli/internal/agent/runner.go      :248, :281, :300, :321, :976
  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go :82  (RunWithConfigViaService bridge — by design)
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go :61, :261, :414, :685
  - cli/internal/agent/grade.go       :169
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go :706
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go :531

Until these are migrated (and in many cases until the function signatures they
flow through are migrated), the AC's grep gate cannot return zero matches.

The fresh-eyes review on the prior attempt itself concluded:

    Path forward: simplest fix is splitting follow-up beads for
    (a) prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
    (b) remaining *_test.go RunOptions sites,
    (c) production-code migration,
    then closing this bead with a narrower AC.

That assessment is correct. The bead title ("5-10 sites") and description ("migrate
test sites … in agent_test.go and any sibling test using RunOptions") describe a
test-scoped refactor; the AC describes a global retirement. The two are not
compatible inside one bead.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED
---------------------------------------
Either:

  Option 1 (preferred): re-cut this bead with a narrowed AC, e.g.

      "rg 'RunOptions\{' cli/internal/agent/{agent,runner,script}_test.go returns
      0 matches in tests that exercise r.Run / RunViaService."

      That AC matches the test-only scope, and the prior commit already satisfies it.

  Option 2: expand bead scope to cover the production migration as well. That is
      a multi-file Stage-2 refactor touching cmd/, internal/server/, internal/exec/,
      and most of internal/agent/. It also requires migrating the signatures of
      BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare, RunViaServiceWith,
      runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, and SessionIndexEntryFromResult so the
      test sites in Class A can shed RunOptions. This belongs in its own bead (or
      epic), not in a "5-10 sites" test-migration bead.

No new code is committed in this attempt because:

  1. The work the bead description actually scopes is already done (5ae02964).
  2. Doing test-only edits that don't move the AC gate would be exploratory churn.
  3. Doing the full production migration here would silently expand the bead scope
     past what the description and title authorize.

Ref: 1da6495, TestReadyExecutionExcludesEpics.\n\nThis is specifically the case that motivated this bead: a continuous-drain worker processed ddx-b44ca394 ('Prevent execute-loop from launching open epics by default') after the epic filter had already been implemented on main. The agent instead made a synthesized no-op commit that only retargeted 17 .claude/skills/helix-* symlinks, which the loop classified as 'success' because a commit was produced. Had no_changes carried a rationale pointing at the prior commit, the loop could have closed the bead as 'already_satisfied' and moved on cleanly.\n\nScope:\n1. Execute-bead prompt contract must explicitly instruct the agent: if you determine the bead's work is already present or cannot be done, report that in a structured way — do NOT make a synthesized commit to 'land something'. Today the prompt says 'Making no commits (no_changes) should be rare' which actively pushes agents toward the wrong behavior.\n2. The agent's exit mechanism needs a way to carry a rationale string back. Simplest version: the agent writes a file to the worktree like .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt before exiting. Gate this behind a 'rationale references existing commit' heuristic to avoid flapping — if the rationale is a vague 'nothing to do', keep current cooldown behavior.\n5. ddx server worker log should render the rationale prominently, not bury it in session metadata.\n6. The prompt contract note that no_changes should be 'rare' is replaced with: 'If the work is already present, say so and provide a reference (commit SHA, test name, file path). .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:390:{"acceptance":"A worker claiming a bead updates execute-loop-heartbeat-at every HeartbeatInterval while the bead is in_progress. A second worker scanning the queue reclaims a bead whose heartbeat is older than HeartbeatTTL and resets it to open before claiming. A bead with no heartbeat and claimed-at older than ClaimTTL is also reclaimable. Conditional write prevents TOCTOU race between two workers both detecting the same stale bead. A structured log entry is written when a stale bead is reclaimed. Existing in_progress beads written before heartbeat was introduced are reclaimable via the ClaimTTL fallback. Tests: (1) worker goroutine killed mid-execution leaves heartbeat timestamp in the past; second worker reclaims it; (2) two workers simultaneously detect a stale bead; only one successfully reclaims it; (3) active worker heartbeat prevents reclaim by another worker.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-13T20:34:21.141792077Z","description":"When an execute-loop worker crashes mid-execution, the bead it was working on is left in status=in_progress indefinitely. The only recovery today is manual: ddx bead unclaim <id>. This blocks the queue from making progress on that bead.

Root cause: cli/internal/bead/store.go Claim() writes claimed-at (RFC3339) and claimed-pid once at claim time and never updates them. There is no heartbeat, no TTL check, and ReadyExecution() in execute_bead_loop.go skips in_progress beads unconditionally.

## Required changes

### 1. Heartbeat writes (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go)
While a bead is in_progress, the owning worker goroutine must periodically update a heartbeat field in bead.Extra. Recommended field name: execute-loop-heartbeat-at (RFC3339). Write interval: every 30s (configurable via HeartbeatInterval on the loop config struct). The heartbeat goroutine runs alongside the agent executor and is cancelled when the bead completes or errors.

### 2. Stale claim detection (cli/internal/bead/store.go or ReadyExecution)
ReadyExecution() currently skips all in_progress beads. Change it to also yield beads where:
  - status == in_progress
  - execute-loop-heartbeat-at is set AND is older than HeartbeatTTL (default: 3 * HeartbeatInterval = 90s)
  - OR claimed-at is older than ClaimTTL (default: 10 minutes) AND execute-loop-heartbeat-at is absent (supports beads claimed before heartbeat was introduced)

When a stale bead is yielded, the claiming worker should first reset status to open via a conditional write (matching the stale heartbeat timestamp to guard against a TOCTOU race) before claiming it as its own. If the conditional write fails, skip and continue scanning.

### 3. Stale-claim log entry
When a worker reclaims a stale bead, write a structured log entry to the worker log: { event: 'stale-claim-reclaimed', bead_id, previous_claimed_machine, previous_claimed_pid, previous_heartbeat_at, stale_for_seconds }.

### 4. Config surface
Expose HeartbeatInterval and ClaimTTL on the loop config. For --local workers, read from .ddx/execute-loop.toml if present. CLI flags: --heartbeat-interval (default 30s) and --claim-ttl (default 10m).

## Key files
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — main loop, claim call at line 124, add heartbeat goroutine
- cli/internal/bead/store.go — Claim() at line 439, ReadyExecution() stale check
- cli/internal/bead/types.go — StatusInProgress constant, Extra field conventions","events_attachment":"ddx-7651506a/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"agent execution failed","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-14T02:45:54Z","id":"ddx-7651506a","issue_type":"task","labels":["bug","execute-loop","concurrency","reliability"],"priority":1,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-c3b314f","status":"closed","title":"Worker heartbeat and stalled-bead TTL reclaim for execute-loop","updated_at":"2026-04-14T04:25:14.996568495Z"}

Its ID is referenced in this bead's events.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-30T00:56:04Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2278885","closing_commit_sha":"e1e5f7cfd8c9656260461a7e63d3e67883bdd77a","created_at":"2026-04-29T19:05:33.7239674Z","description":"## Two-layer problem

### Layer 1 (mechanical bug)

When a worker invokes `ddx bead create` from inside its execute-bead worktree, the resulting bead IDs are malformed. Reproduced today on this project:

  /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx bead ready  → 11 entries with IDs like
  `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-44236615-20260429T185004-ffa9adf1-e630aeae`

These were the read-coverage child beads spawned from the ddx-44236615 worker (commit ebeb2464). They have valid descriptions, AC, priorities, and labels — only the ID field is mangled. The pattern `.execute-bead-wt-<parent>-<timestamp>-<short>-<random>` matches the worktree directory name format used by `ddx agent execute-bead`. Most likely failure path: the bead-id resolver consults `os.Getwd()` (or equivalent) to derive a project context prefix, sees the worktree dir, and uses it instead of the real project root + a fresh `ddx-<8hex>` short hash.

Concrete impact:
- 50+ char IDs are unwieldy in CLI commands (`ddx bead show <id>`, `ddx bead dep add ...`).
- Cross-project readers and the bead store assume `ddx-<8hex>` prefix matching for tooling (filtering, GraphQL adapters, MCP responses) — these long IDs may sort or filter unexpectedly.
- The IDs encode ephemeral attempt timestamps, which leaks worker-internal state into a permanent identifier.
- 12 such beads exist in the tracker as of this filing (grep `.execute-bead-wt-` in `.ddx/beads.jsonl`).

### Layer 2 (design question — separate decision)

Even if the IDs were correctly formed `ddx-<8hex>`, it is not obvious that `ddx bead create` from inside an execute-bead worker is the right pattern at all. Two alternatives the design has not articulated a position on:

**(a) Append to the parent bead.** When the worker discovers sub-tasks, it appends them as structured items in the parent's description, notes, or a new `kind:discovered-subtask` event. The parent bead becomes the durable record of "what this run found"; future runs (or the operator) can decompose if/when the work warrants its own tracker entries.

**(b) Surface via the execute-bead result.** The agent emits a structured `discovered_subtasks: [...]` field on result.json; the loop / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.

**(c) Create new beads in-band (current behavior).** The worker calls `ddx bead create` directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue if the agent over-decomposes; complicates "what was THIS run's contribution" auditing.

Today's behavior is (c) but undocumented. The 11 read-coverage children landing as a single agent's decomposition is exactly the failure mode (c) makes easy: one analysis bead spawned 11 implementation beads in a single run, all P0, all without operator review. The work may be valid — but the pattern needs a position.

## In-scope outcomes

This bead's job is to surface both layers; the implementation work decomposes into follow-ups.

1. **Concrete fix for layer 1**: bead-id resolver should normalize worktree paths to the real project root before allocating an ID. Test: `ddx bead create` invoked with cwd=`<repo>/.execute-bead-wt-...` produces `ddx-<8hex>`, not `.execute-bead-wt-...-<random>`.
2. **Mass repair for the 12 existing malformed beads**: either rewrite their IDs to fresh `ddx-<8hex>` (with a redirect or alias map for any external references), or close them as deferred and re-file with proper IDs. Decision deferred until layer 2 is settled.
3. **Layer 2 decision**: file a separate design bead (or amendment to FEAT-006 / FEAT-010) that picks (a), (b), or (c) and documents the rationale. This bead does NOT pre-decide; it captures the question.

## In-scope files (layer 1 fix)

- `cli/cmd/bead_create.go` (or wherever bead-create resolves project context).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` or `cli/internal/bead/id.go` if ID generation lives there.
- A unit test that drops cwd into a worktree-shaped dir and asserts the resulting bead id matches `^ddx-[0-9a-f]{8}$`.

(Locate via: `grep -rn "execute-bead-wt\|FindProjectRoot\|projectRoot" cli/internal/bead/ cli/cmd/bead_*.go`.)

## Out of scope

- The 11 read-coverage child beads' actual implementation work — that's separate beads.
- Default behavior change for whether workers can/should create beads — that's the layer-2 design bead.
- GraphQL or MCP changes to handle long IDs — fix the source instead.

## Acceptance

Layer-1 only (this bead):

1. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/...` passes after the fix.
2. New unit test in `cli/internal/bead/` (or `cli/cmd/`) that simulates a bead-create call from `cwd=<project>/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-XXXXX-<timestamp>-<random>` and asserts the new bead's `id` field matches `^ddx-[0-9a-f]{8}$`. Test fails before the fix, passes after.
3. `grep -E '"id":"\.execute-bead-wt-' .ddx/beads.jsonl` returns either 0 (mass repair) or 12 with a recorded decision in this bead's notes that the existing 12 are deferred to a follow-up bead.
4. A separate bead is filed (or referenced existing one updated) that captures the layer-2 design question (a/b/c). Reference its ID in this bead's events.

## Why one bead, not an epic

Layer 1 is a tight 1-3 file fix. Layer 2 is a design discussion that should be its own artifact, not a sibling task. Splitting them keeps each bounded.","events_attachment":"ddx-7eab13a6/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-30T00:59:34.510624284Z","id":"ddx-7eab13a6","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:bead","area:agent","kind:bug","kind:design"],"notes":"Layer-1 (ID resolver bug) fixed in this bead: detectPrefix() now takes a workingDir parameter and uses it for git commands instead of defaulting to cwd. This prevents linked worktrees from contaminating the prefix.

22 existing malformed beads with .execute-bead-wt-* IDs are DEFERRED to a mass-repair follow-up. Decision pending on layer-2 design question (ddx-d3a4f89c) — once the in-band vs. append vs. result-field question is decided, the repair approach will be clearer. The malformed beads have valid descriptions, AC, priorities, and labels; only their IDs are wrong.

Fix in store.go correctly threads workingDir through detectPrefix and resolves the worktree-cwd contamination. However, the new test does not simulate the bug scenario: it never chdirs into a worktree-shaped path, so it would pass on main too — failing AC #2's 'fails-before-fix, passes-after' requirement. Layer-2 bead and deferral note are acceptable.","owner":"erik","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-22cc4abe","status":"closed","title":"bead-id resolver: malformed IDs when ddx bead create runs from inside an execute-bead worktree (+ flag whether workers should create beads in-band at all)","updated_at":"2026-04-30T01:01:23.445619428Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:566:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer browsing the web UI beads view, I want clicking a bead to open its detail pane within ~200ms on a repo with hundreds of beads across several projects, so the UI feels interactive.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. A benchmark/harness exists (Go benchmark or scripted GraphQL client) that measures end-to-end `bead(id:)` latency against a fixture repo with ≥ 5 projects and ≥ 500 beads total. Output is a p50/p95 number, not a pass/fail bool.
2. Baseline is recorded in the bead notes (actual number measured on the current `main` binary at the time of fix).
3. After the fix, p95 for `bead(id:)` on the fixture is ≤ 50ms in-process / ≤ 200ms over HTTP on a dev laptop.
4. The fix does not introduce a stale-read regression: writing a bead via `ddx bead update` and then fetching it via GraphQL returns the new value on the next request (verified by a test that does write-then-read).
5. Concurrency-safe: an integration test spawns N concurrent `bead(id:)` queries interleaved with `bead update` mutations and asserts no panics, no torn reads, and consistent responses.
6. Playwright e2e: open `/nodes/.../beads`, click a bead row, assert the sidepane shows the bead title within 1s (generous budget; guards against regressions not the p95).","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"c1822352b4bbfa4181f96a203bce522d249f342d","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:07:18.806879855Z","description":"## Observed

Opening a bead in the sidepane on `/nodes/.../projects/.../beads?q=test` takes 5+ seconds. The beads list renders quickly; the delay is on single-bead lookup.

## Root cause

`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver.go:93-100` — the `Bead(id)` resolver calls `r.State.GetBeadSnapshots("", "", "", "")` with no filters, which at `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:49-105` walks **every project**, constructs a `bead.Store` per project, calls `ReadAll()` (full directory scan + JSONL parse of every bead file), then iterates the result in Go to find one matching ID.

So every sidepane open = `O(projects × beads_per_project)` disk reads + parses, for a lookup that should be `O(1)` or `O(log n)`.

`bead.Store` already exposes `Get(id)` (`cli/internal/bead/store.go:450`), but the resolver doesn't use it — nor does it know which project the bead lives in, because the GraphQL schema exposes `bead(id:)` without a project scope.

## Proposed direction (not prescriptive)

Any of these would resolve it; pick based on measurements:

1. **Project scope the query.** Change schema to `bead(projectId:, id:)` (or accept a composite global id) and call `Store.Get` directly.
2. **Maintain an in-memory index** `beadID → projectID` refreshed on bead mutations and on a debounced filesystem watcher; resolver does one map lookup + one `Store.Get`.
3. **Short-TTL snapshot cache** behind `GetBeadSnapshots`, invalidated on bead writes.

Before picking: add a baseline measurement harness (see acceptance) so we know the actual p50/p95 before and after, not just anecdote.","events_attachment":"ddx-ad0db8fd/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model "minimax/minimax-m2.7" for harness "gemini"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"feature":"FEAT-008","id":"ddx-ad0db8fd","issue_type":"bug","labels":["feat-008","feat-004","perf","graphql"],"notes":"Baseline/after measurement for bead(id:) lookup fixture (5 projects, 500 total beads, go test ./internal/server -run ^$ -bench "BenchmarkGraphQLBeadLookup(LegacyScan)?Latency$" -benchtime=80x -count=1, linux/arm64):

`ddx agent execute-loop` selected and launched an open `epic` bead from the HELIX execution queue instead of a bounded executable bead.

## Reproduction
1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx bead ready --json --execution`.
2. Observe the first candidate is `helix-fef22846`, `issue_type=epic`, title `Audit deleted HELIX artifact types for restoration or retirement`.
3. Run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json`.
4. Observe DDx creates `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7` and `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json` with `bead_id=helix-fef22846`.

## Observed gap
`ReadyExecution()` currently treats any open, unblocked bead as execution-ready unless `execution-eligible=false`, `superseded-by`, or cooldown metadata says otherwise. `execute-loop` then takes the first candidate without a stronger structural execution screen. That behavior is weaker than the API-001 execute-bead supervisor contract, which says the loop should run a generic execution-ready validator against ordered candidates before launch.

## Why this matters
Open epics represent umbrella planning/decomposition work, not necessarily a bounded workspace transform. Launching them directly makes queue-drain behavior unstable and can strand the loop on non-executable work even when bounded tasks are ready behind the epic.","events_attachment":"ddx-b44ca394/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T03:39:25.89063588Z","execute-loop-last-detail":"agent execution failed","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-13T21:32:10Z","execution-eligible":true,"id":"ddx-b44ca394","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","phase:build","area:agent","area:bead","area:cli"],"notes":"Blocker ddx-cf340665 is closed. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:830:{"acceptance":"1. Per-field hard cap of 65,535 bytes on bead.events[].body and adjacent writer paths, documented in cli/internal/bead package comment. bd-compatibility rationale in the comment.
2. Reviewer raw streams never enter bead.events[].body. Reviewer output path writes the full stream to .ddx/executions/<id>/reviewer-stream.log; event body is ≤512 bytes (verdict + first-line rationale + path reference). Unit test: a 2MB reviewer stream produces a <=512-byte event body referencing the full payload on disk.
3. Other writers that append to bead.events[].body enforce the per-field cap; overflow persists as an artifact under .ddx/executions/<id>/ and the event body carries a path reference plus summary.
4. Reader: bead/session scanner buffers raised from the current 1MB to at least 16MB (covers observed extremes: 1.46MB bead row, ~7MB session rows). No new global "shared buffer constant" abstraction — raise each call site's existing Buffer() arg (cli/internal/bead/store.go:172, processmetrics.go:723, session_log_tailer.go:91). claude_stream.go:101 is already 16MB.
5. Regression test: a synthetic beads.jsonl line of 2MB reads cleanly; a line exceeding the buffer surfaces an error naming the bead id and does not crash.
6. bd round-trip smoke: a DDx-authored bead with one 65,535-byte description field exports to JSONL and is accepted by `bd import`. Requires bd on PATH; otherwise t.Skip() with a clear message.
7. Migration / recovery: corrupted rows encountered during read fall back to best-effort parse (truncated body + warning); do NOT mutate existing tracker files in-place. A separate `ddx bead repair` bead can be filed if real corruption is found in the wild.
8. cd cli && go test ./... green.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-20T13:55:31.696168117Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-f8a11202","depends_on_id":"ddx-004eccbc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:04:11Z"}],"description":"Reporter: nexiq+axon 2026-04-18/19/20. Symptom 2 of review-malfunction report.

A reviewer emitted a ~1.46MB event body (streamed reviewer output dumped verbatim into bead.events[].body). Because cli/internal/bead/store.go:172 sets bufio.Scanner.Buffer to 1MB, subsequent reads of beads.jsonl fail with "bufio.Scanner: token too long." Tracker effectively bricked until the row is hand-trimmed.

## Compatibility constraint (validated 2026-04-20)

Empirical bd test (/tmp/bd-size-test via homebrew bd 1.0.2):
- Per-field insert cap: 65,535 bytes. Exactly Dolt TEXT column limit. 65535 inserts; 65536 errors "string too long."
- Per-line export: easily exceeds 1MB when a bead accumulates multiple long comments/events. Observed: one bead with 5 × 65KB comments → 326KB line. Our 1.46MB corruption is ~23 such events, plausible for a long-running bead.
- bd export lines ARE currently unreadable by DDx when they exceed our 1MB scanner cap — even when no single field is oversized.

## Design contract

- Write-side (DDx): enforce per-field hard cap of 65,535 bytes so DDx-authored beads round-trip through `bd import`. Soft cap ~10KB with head+tail+marker truncation for typical event bodies. Overflow persists as an artifact file under .ddx/executions/<id>/, event body stores path + one-line summary.
- Read-side (DDx): accept arbitrarily long lines from bd-compatible tools. Scanner buffer sized generously (suggest 64MB) so normal accumulated beads never trigger token-too-long.
- Read-side tolerance: if a line does exceed the buffer, surface a clear error with bead id and pointer to the artifact, do not crash.

## Known scanner sites

All currently undersized relative to bd export reality:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:172 (1MB)
- cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:723 (1MB)
- cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:101 (16MB — probably OK)
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_tailer.go:91 (4MB)

## Artifact reference

.ddx/executions/20260505T184548-9ec8fec6/manifest.json:12:    "description": "PROBLEM
The queue contained multiple generated child beads whose IDs started with .execute-bead-wt-..., for example .execute-bead-wt-ddx-8d747049-20260505T023636-7669921a-b6bc9398 and .execute-bead-wt-ddx-c8f79963-20260505T104148-6e54f8ef-8083652e. Those IDs are not valid execute-bead git ref path components and one attempt already failed while preserving refs for such an ID.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/bead/worktree_prefix_test.go:13-27 and cli/cmd/bead_workspace_test.go:42-84 cover in-process store/command factory paths, but the observed failure came from autonomous decomposition running ddx bead create inside an actual execute-bead linked worktree.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1818-1841 derives prefixes from the store working directory and git root, but the real installed-binary/worktree path is not covered by an end-to-end test that creates a linked worktree and runs ddx bead create as a subprocess.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a real integration regression test that builds or invokes the current ddx test binary from inside an execute-bead-shaped linked worktree with DDX_BEAD_DIR=.ddx, creates a child bead, and verifies the child ID and storage location use the origin workspace naming convention. If the test fails, fix the command/store workspace resolution path so child beads are created as ddx-* IDs in the primary workspace store.

NON-SCOPE
- Cleaning historical malformed tracker entries; the queue-quality review already superseded the open queued copies.
- Changing the execute-bead worktree naming convention itself.",

2. The same test asserts the bead row is written to the primary workspace .ddx/beads.jsonl, not the isolated worktree copy.
3. The regression test fails if the ID begins with .execute-bead-wt-.
4. If current code fails the test, fix workspace/store prefix resolution so the test passes for real subprocess execution.

The store factory still treats Axon as an experimental special case and only enables it behind an environment gate. The relevant branch is cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137, which falls back to JSONL unless DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy, even when config has backend: axon. The checked-in sample config also does not document the bead_tracker backend block yet (.ddx/config.yaml:1-17).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137 and .ddx/config.yaml:1-17 do not present Axon as a normal selectable backend. The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.
- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.
- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.
- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Axon backend CRUD internals.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:234:{"acceptance":"1. .ddx/config.yaml documents bead_tracker.backend: axon plus the Axon connection block.
2. TestNewStore_DefaultsToJSONL, TestNewStore_SelectsAxonFromConfig, and TestNewStore_PreservesExternalBackends are added or updated.
3. config selection honors axon without requiring DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL, while jsonl remains the default.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-05T16:19:59.861362738Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-29f02cf4","depends_on_id":"ddx-9c5bca8f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-05T16:20:15Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
The store factory still treats Axon as an experimental special case and only enables it behind an environment gate. The relevant branch is cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137, which falls back to JSONL unless DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy, even when config has backend: axon. The checked-in sample config also does not document the bead_tracker backend block yet (.ddx/config.yaml:1-17).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137 and .ddx/config.yaml:1-17 do not present Axon as a normal selectable backend. The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.
- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.
- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.
- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Axon backend CRUD internals.
- Subscription transport.","id":"ddx-29f02cf4","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:beads","area:storage","kind:feature","blocked-on-upstream:axon-05c1019d","blocked-on-upstream:axon-82b6f7b2"],"parent":"ddx-8d747049","priority":0,"status":"open","title":"bead/axon: wire backend selection through .ddx config","updated_at":"2026-05-05T16:20:15.586803055Z"}

rationale: no_changes: implementation for ADR-022 step 3 (operator-cancel via bead-store
marker + mid-attempt poll) is already present in the repository at base-rev
9b17c6e7. It landed via merge 48684e63 of feature commit 996e01f7
("feat(agent): operator-cancel via bead-store marker + mid-attempt poll
[ddx-2e54b560]"). The prior REVIEW:BLOCK observed only an execution-evidence
result.json in that attempt's diff because the source code had already merged
on the preceding attempt; nothing more was left to commit.

Acceptance criteria mapped to evidence in the current worktree:

  AC1 — POST /api/beads/<id>/cancel writes extra.cancel-requested:true:
        cli/internal/server/server.go (handleCancelBead);
        cli/internal/bead/store.go (RequestCancel).

  AC2 — Worker mid-attempt loop polls bead extra every 10s:
        cli/internal/agent/cancel_poll.go (CancelPollInterval = 10s,
        startCancelPoller); wired in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go.

  AC3 — On cancel-requested, worker emits preserved_for_review with reason
        "operator_cancel"; cancel-honored:true is set:
        cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go (OperatorCancelReason,
        ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview); poll loop in
        cli/internal/agent/cancel_poll.go writes cancel-honored via
        bead.Store.HonorCancel.

  AC4 — Idempotent (second cancel is silent no-op once cancel-honored is set):
        cli/internal/bead/store.go (RequestCancel short-circuits when
        cancel-honored is already true).

  AC5 — Tests TestOperatorCancel_SetsBeadExtra,
        TestWorker_HonorsCancelMidAttempt, TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA:
        cli/internal/server/server_cancel_test.go:14;
        cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_cancel_test.go:99 and :166.

  AC6 — go test green; lefthook pre-commit passes (verification command
        below re-runs the cancel-related tests).

  AC7 — WIRED-IN integration test TestOperatorCancel_DuringRealClaudeAttempt
        uses a real *bead.Store + RequestCancel and a blocking runner that
        only returns when its dispatch context is cancelled — proving the
        cancel travels the live signal path, not a stub:
        cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_cancel_test.go:237.

The only working-tree delta vs base-rev is an unrelated deletion of
.agents/skills/sample-skill, which is outside this bead's scope and is
intentionally left unstaged.

Result: no changes needed; AC already satisfied by prior commits.

Audit against acceptance criteria
---------------------------------

AC1. New collection abstraction lives in cli/internal/bead and store.go uses it for the JSONL path.

  - cli/internal/bead/registry.go defines CollectionID, CollectionSpec, and
    Registry. CollectionSpec exposes FileName(), LockName(), and PathsUnder()
    (registry.go:38-58). DefaultRegistry() ships "beads" and "beads-archive"
    specs (registry.go:119-132).
  - store.go consumes the abstraction at NewStore: after option processing it
    resolves the collection via DefaultRegistry().Resolve(CollectionID(s.Collection))
    and assigns s.File / s.LockDir from spec.PathsUnder(dir) (store.go:94-95).
    The Store struct carries Collection, and WithCollection /
    NewStoreWithCollection are the public knobs (store.go:40-119).

AC2. Default 'beads' collection produces the same .ddx/beads.jsonl path it does today.

  - TestStoreUsesRegistrySpecForBeads verifies NewStore(.ddx) yields
    Collection="beads", File=".ddx/beads.jsonl", LockDir=".ddx/beads.lock"
    (registry_test.go:47-53). PASS.
  - TestRegistryResolveUsesRegisteredSpec confirms the registered "beads"
    spec returns "beads.jsonl" / "beads.lock" (registry_test.go:20-25). PASS.

AC3. Opening a non-'beads' collection on the JSONL backend reads/writes .ddx/<name>.jsonl.

  - Registry.Resolve synthesizes a conventional spec for any unregistered
    name (registry.go:96-101).
  - TestInitUsesCollectionFile (store_test.go:49-58) opens
    NewStoreWithCollection(".ddx", "exec-runs") and asserts
    File=".ddx/exec-runs.jsonl", LockDir=".ddx/exec-runs.lock", and that Init
    creates the file. PASS.
  - TestWithCollectionNormalizesJSONLExtension (store_test.go:61-66) shows
    WithCollection("agent-sessions.jsonl") strips the suffix and routes to
    .ddx/agent-sessions.jsonl. PASS.
  - TestRegistryResolveSynthesizesUnknownCollection (registry_test.go:27-32)
    proves the abstraction is uniform for arbitrary names.

AC4. All existing tests under cli/internal/bead and cli/cmd pass unchanged.

  - go test ./internal/bead/... → ok (4.995s).
  - cli/cmd has preexisting failures (TestReviewEvidenceApproveAttributesToTier,
    TestAcceptance_US028..US034, TestInstallationPerformance). I confirmed
    these fail identically on the unmodified base commit 55838f32 (git stash
    + run + pop), so they are environmental / preexisting and out of scope
    for this bead.

AC5. schema_compat_test.go still green; bd/br interchange unchanged for 'beads'.

  - The full ./internal/bead/... suite (which includes
    schema_compat_test.go, schema_validation_test.go, and the external-backend
    branch coverage in store_test.go:82-100) passes. ExternalBackend continues
    to take a Collection argument from NewStore but defaults to "beads" via
    DefaultCollection (store.go:84, backend_external.go:14-22), preserving
    bd/br interchange for the active queue.

Why nothing was committed
-------------------------

The bead description says store.go "currently hard-codes .ddx/beads.jsonl".
That premise is stale: prior beads in the ADR-004 decomposition (parent
ddx-0c0565f3) already shipped the refactor:

  - 84c1881d  refactor(bead): introduce collection registry  [ddx-9cc3abad]
  - 6b997f39  feat(bead): add beads-archive collection ...    [ddx-9aecc66c]
  - dcd29dd5  feat(bead): externalize closed-bead events ...  [ddx-5d67c121]
  - a16114f1  feat(bead): add migrate command ...             [ddx-c4476899]

Every AC item maps to existing code and a passing test (cited above).
Adding a duplicate abstraction or renaming CollectionSpec → CollectionRef
would be churn without behavioral value and would risk the AC4 invariant
("existing tests pass unchanged"). DOWITYTD applies.

Recommended follow-up: close ddx-2f453147 as already-satisfied with a note
linking to ddx-9cc3abad.

  - TestRegistryResolveUsesRegisteredSpec confirms the registered "beads"
    spec returns "beads.jsonl" / "beads.lock" (registry_test.go:20-25). PASS.

AC3. Opening a non-'beads' collection on the JSONL backend reads/writes .ddx/<name>.jsonl.

  - Registry.Resolve synthesizes a conventional spec for any unregistered
    name (registry.go:96-101).
  - TestInitUsesCollectionFile (store_test.go:49-58) opens
    NewStoreWithCollection(".ddx", "exec-runs") and asserts
    File=".ddx/exec-runs.jsonl", LockDir=".ddx/exec-runs.lock", and that Init
    creates the file. PASS.
  - TestWithCollectionNormalizesJSONLExtension (store_test.go:61-66) shows
    WithCollection("agent-sessions.jsonl") strips the suffix and routes to
    .ddx/agent-sessions.jsonl. PASS.
  - TestRegistryResolveSynthesizesUnknownCollection (registry_test.go:27-32)
    proves the abstraction is uniform for arbitrary names.

AC4. All existing tests under cli/internal/bead and cli/cmd pass unchanged.

  - go test ./internal/bead/... → ok (4.995s).
  - cli/cmd has preexisting failures (TestReviewEvidenceApproveAttributesToTier,
    TestAcceptance_US028..US034, TestInstallationPerformance). I confirmed
    these fail identically on the unmodified base commit 55838f32 (git stash
    + run + pop), so they are environmental / preexisting and out of scope
    for this bead.

AC5. schema_compat_test.go still green; bd/br interchange unchanged for 'beads'.

  - The full ./internal/bead/... suite (which includes
    schema_compat_test.go, schema_validation_test.go, and the external-backend
    branch coverage in store_test.go:82-100) passes. ExternalBackend continues
    to take a Collection argument from NewStore but defaults to "beads" via
    DefaultCollection (store.go:84, backend_external.go:14-22), preserving
    bd/br interchange for the active queue.

Why nothing was committed
-------------------------

The bead description says store.go "currently hard-codes .ddx/beads.jsonl".
That premise is stale: prior beads in the ADR-004 decomposition (parent
ddx-0c0565f3) already shipped the refactor:

  - 84c1881d  refactor(bead): introduce collection registry  [ddx-9cc3abad]
  - 6b997f39  feat(bead): add beads-archive collection ...    [ddx-9aecc66c]
  - dcd29dd5  feat(bead): externalize closed-bead events ...  [ddx-5d67c121]
  - a16114f1  feat(bead): add migrate command ...             [ddx-c4476899]

Every AC item maps to existing code and a passing test (cited above).
Adding a duplicate abstraction or renaming CollectionSpec → CollectionRef
would be churn without behavioral value and would risk the AC4 invariant
("existing tests pass unchanged"). DOWITYTD applies.

Recommended follow-up: close ddx-2f453147 as already-satisfied with a note
linking to ddx-9cc3abad.

That's useless for triage — the operator cannot tell whether:\n- the bead is genuinely already satisfied (work present in a prior commit)\n- the agent gave up because it couldn't find the relevant files\n- the agent classified the task as ambiguous or too risky\n- the agent hit a tool limit or context cap\n- the prompt contract was unclear\n\nThe fix is to make no_changes a structured result that carries a short rationale from the agent itself, written at the point where the agent decided not to commit. Example of what the operator should see:\n\n  status:    no_changes\n  rationale: The epic filter requested by this bead was already added in\n             commit 1da6495 (cli/internal/bead/store.go readyFiltered). I\n             reviewed the current code and tests, confirmed the filter\n             excludes 'epic' beads from ReadyExecution, and verified\n             TestReadyExecutionExcludesEpics exists and passes. Nothing\n             to do. Ref: 1da6495, TestReadyExecutionExcludesEpics.\n\nThis is specifically the case that motivated this bead: a continuous-drain worker processed ddx-b44ca394 ('Prevent execute-loop from launching open epics by default') after the epic filter had already been implemented on main. The agent instead made a synthesized no-op commit that only retargeted 17 .claude/skills/helix-* symlinks, which the loop classified as 'success' because a commit was produced. Had no_changes carried a rationale pointing at the prior commit, the loop could have closed the bead as 'already_satisfied' and moved on cleanly.\n\nScope:\n1. Execute-bead prompt contract must explicitly instruct the agent: if you determine the bead's work is already present or cannot be done, report that in a structured way — do NOT make a synthesized commit to 'land something'. Today the prompt says 'Making no commits (no_changes) should be rare' which actively pushes agents toward the wrong behavior.\n2. The agent's exit mechanism needs a way to carry a rationale string back. Simplest version: the agent writes a file to the worktree like .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt before exiting. ExecuteBead reads it if no commits were produced and attaches the text to the result.NoChangesRationale field.\n3. The ExecuteBeadResult struct gains a NoChangesRationale string field, populated only when outcome == no_changes. Status: success and the unused 'reason' field need not change.\n4. execute-loop close semantics: when no_changes arrives with a non-empty rationale pointing at a prior commit, the loop can optionally close the bead as already_satisfied with the rationale recorded in the close event body (instead of waiting for MaxNoChangesBeforeClose=3 strikes). Gate this behind a 'rationale references existing commit' heuristic to avoid flapping — if the rationale is a vague 'nothing to do', keep current cooldown behavior.\n5. ddx server worker log should render the rationale prominently, not bury it in session metadata.\n6. The prompt contract note that no_changes should be 'rare' is replaced with: 'If the work is already present, say so and provide a reference (commit SHA, test name, file path). A well-justified no_changes is always preferred to a cosmetic synthesized commit.'","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"success

Operators need ddx work and queue diagnostics to explain why a dependency-ready bead is or is not execution-ready. Today cooldown can mask distinct lifecycle reasons such as non-executable parent/epic containers, superseded work, blocked status/labels, execution-eligible=false, or active retry-after. That creates delay because the queue looks time-blocked instead of actionably blocked.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1338-1384 ReadyExecutionBreakdown reports SkippedEpics, SkippedOnCooldown, SkippedNotEligible, SkippedSuperseded, and NextRetryAfter, but the surrounding queue behavior and diagnostics are not yet explicitly tied to TD-031 lifecycle reasons.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1390-1450 readyFiltered filters execution-eligible, superseded-by, and retry cooldown for executionOnly, but it does not clearly handle persisted blocked/proposed/cancelled statuses beyond StatusOpen and stale in_progress logic.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1478-1536 BlockedAll reports dependency and retry cooldown blockers, but durable non-executable reasons are not surfaced as first-class blocked/skip diagnostics.
- cli/cmd/bead.go:869-946 and cli/cmd/work_plan.go present queue views; they need to distinguish cooldown from non-executable lifecycle reasons so operators know what action is required.

PROPOSED FIX
- Align execution-ready queue derivation and diagnostics with TD-031, using existing status, labels, Extra fields, parent/type, dependency, and superseded metadata.
- Preserve broad ddx bead ready semantics if it intentionally shows dependency-ready beads; make execution-ready/work-plan output reflect what ddx work can pick.
- Ensure ordinary ddx work does not pick open epics/parents unless an epic-worker mode explicitly owns them.
- Surface distinct skip categories: active cooldown, not executable, superseded, persisted blocked, epic-only, and dependency-blocked.
- Keep the skip explanation durable and explainable; do not introduce new statuses.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing no_changes action application in execute loop; ddx-fd1766e5 owns that.
- Reconciliation of existing stale tracker metadata; follow-up bead owns that.
- UI changes beyond CLI diagnostics.
- Epic-worker implementation.

PARENT
- ddx-a9d130d0.

DEPS
- ddx-07e6d2fa: TD-031 must define the execution-skip invariants.
- ddx-fd1766e5: queue diagnostics should reflect the fields live execution now applies.

INTERSECTIONS
- Closed ddx-af14e457 fixed execution eligibility selection once; this bead must preserve that behavior and add diagnostics rather than regress it.
- API-002 epic worker surfaces already define separate epic execution semantics; this bead only keeps ordinary ddx work from treating epics as single-ticket tasks.","id":"ddx-49eeb476","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:beads","area:cli","kind:fix","reliability","bead-lifecycle","spec:TD-031"],"parent":"ddx-a9d130d0","priority":1,"spec-id":"TD-031","status":"open","title":"beads: explain TD-031 execution-ready skip reasons","updated_at":"2026-05-05T20:43:57.306046048Z"}

The no_changes/cooldown pile-up involved several interacting paths: try adjudication, live execute-loop store mutations, queue derivation, and reconciliation of stale Extra fields. Unit tests on each piece are necessary but not sufficient to prevent regressions where the pieces drift apart again.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go can prove an action classification, but it does not prove execute-loop store mutation or queue eligibility.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go can prove live mutation, but it does not prove the later queue view explains the resulting state correctly.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go queue tests can prove filtering, but not that real no_changes outcomes produce the durable fields queue filtering expects.
- There is no fixture suite built around the real pile-up patterns: verified already done, latest success with stale no_changes Extra, parent/epic already decomposed, external blocker, unjustified no rationale, and transient retryable cooldown.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small cross-path regression suite using existing in-memory/temp-repo test helpers.
- Cover each observed pile-up class from input attempt/event through durable tracker state and execution-ready diagnostics.
- Keep tests focused; do not add a broad e2e that shells out to real agents.
- Reuse the TD-031 action semantics and queue diagnostic helpers implemented by dependency beads.

NON-SCOPE
- New production behavior beyond fixing test seams needed to observe existing behavior.
- Playwright/UI coverage.
- Real provider/harness calls.
- Historical tracker mutation outside test fixtures.

PARENT
- ddx-a9d130d0.

DEPS
- ddx-fd1766e5: live no_changes action application must exist.
- ddx-49eeb476: execution-ready diagnostics must expose durable skip reasons.
- ddx-837a2254: reconciliation command/pass must exist for stale tracker fixtures.

INTERSECTIONS
- This bead should move any regression fixture that would otherwise duplicate implementation-bead tests into the narrowest appropriate package, but it owns the cross-path coverage that spans multiple packages.","id":"ddx-75e1f4a5","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:test","area:agent","area:beads","kind:test","reliability","bead-lifecycle","spec:TD-031"],"parent":"ddx-a9d130d0","priority":2,"spec-id":"TD-031","status":"open","title":"test: lock TD-031 no_changes lifecycle regression fixtures","updated_at":"2026-05-05T20:44:46.965914107Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:557:{"acceptance":"A worker claiming a bead updates execute-loop-heartbeat-at every HeartbeatInterval while the bead is in_progress. A second worker scanning the queue reclaims a bead whose heartbeat is older than HeartbeatTTL and resets it to open before claiming. A bead with no heartbeat and claimed-at older than ClaimTTL is also reclaimable. Conditional write prevents TOCTOU race between two workers both detecting the same stale bead. A structured log entry is written when a stale bead is reclaimed. Existing in_progress beads written before heartbeat was introduced are reclaimable via the ClaimTTL fallback. Tests: (1) worker goroutine killed mid-execution leaves heartbeat timestamp in the past; second worker reclaims it; (2) two workers simultaneously detect a stale bead; only one successfully reclaims it; (3) active worker heartbeat prevents reclaim by another worker.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-13T20:34:21.141792077Z","description":"When an execute-loop worker crashes mid-execution, the bead it was working on is left in status=in_progress indefinitely. The only recovery today is manual: ddx bead unclaim <id>. This blocks the queue from making progress on that bead.

Root cause: cli/internal/bead/store.go Claim() writes claimed-at (RFC3339) and claimed-pid once at claim time and never updates them. There is no heartbeat, no TTL check, and ReadyExecution() in execute_bead_loop.go skips in_progress beads unconditionally.

## Required changes

### 1. Heartbeat writes (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go)
While a bead is in_progress, the owning worker goroutine must periodically update a heartbeat field in bead.Extra. Recommended field name: execute-loop-heartbeat-at (RFC3339). Write interval: every 30s (configurable via HeartbeatInterval on the loop config struct). The heartbeat goroutine runs alongside the agent executor and is cancelled when the bead completes or errors.

### 2. Stale claim detection (cli/internal/bead/store.go or ReadyExecution)
ReadyExecution() currently skips all in_progress beads. Change it to also yield beads where:
  - status == in_progress
  - execute-loop-heartbeat-at is set AND is older than HeartbeatTTL (default: 3 * HeartbeatInterval = 90s)
  - OR claimed-at is older than ClaimTTL (default: 10 minutes) AND execute-loop-heartbeat-at is absent (supports beads claimed before heartbeat was introduced)

When a stale bead is yielded, the claiming worker should first reset status to open via a conditional write (matching the stale heartbeat timestamp to guard against a TOCTOU race) before claiming it as its own. If the conditional write fails, skip and continue scanning.

### 3. Stale-claim log entry
When a worker reclaims a stale bead, write a structured log entry to the worker log: { event: 'stale-claim-reclaimed', bead_id, previous_claimed_machine, previous_claimed_pid, previous_heartbeat_at, stale_for_seconds }.

### 4. Config surface
Expose HeartbeatInterval and ClaimTTL on the loop config. For --local workers, read from .ddx/execute-loop.toml if present. CLI flags: --heartbeat-interval (default 30s) and --claim-ttl (default 10m).

## Key files
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — main loop, claim call at line 124, add heartbeat goroutine
- cli/internal/bead/store.go — Claim() at line 439, ReadyExecution() stale check
- cli/internal/bead/types.go — StatusInProgress constant, Extra field conventions","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"agent execution failed

Its ID is referenced in this bead's events.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-30T00:56:04Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2278885","closing_commit_sha":"e1e5f7cfd8c9656260461a7e63d3e67883bdd77a","created_at":"2026-04-29T19:05:33.7239674Z","description":"## Two-layer problem

### Layer 1 (mechanical bug)

When a worker invokes `ddx bead create` from inside its execute-bead worktree, the resulting bead IDs are malformed. Reproduced today on this project:

  /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx bead ready  → 11 entries with IDs like
  `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-44236615-20260429T185004-ffa9adf1-e630aeae`

These were the read-coverage child beads spawned from the ddx-44236615 worker (commit ebeb2464). They have valid descriptions, AC, priorities, and labels — only the ID field is mangled. The pattern `.execute-bead-wt-<parent>-<timestamp>-<short>-<random>` matches the worktree directory name format used by `ddx agent execute-bead`. Most likely failure path: the bead-id resolver consults `os.Getwd()` (or equivalent) to derive a project context prefix, sees the worktree dir, and uses it instead of the real project root + a fresh `ddx-<8hex>` short hash.

Concrete impact:
- 50+ char IDs are unwieldy in CLI commands (`ddx bead show <id>`, `ddx bead dep add ...`).
- Cross-project readers and the bead store assume `ddx-<8hex>` prefix matching for tooling (filtering, GraphQL adapters, MCP responses) — these long IDs may sort or filter unexpectedly.
- The IDs encode ephemeral attempt timestamps, which leaks worker-internal state into a permanent identifier.
- 12 such beads exist in the tracker as of this filing (grep `.execute-bead-wt-` in `.ddx/beads.jsonl`).

### Layer 2 (design question — separate decision)

Even if the IDs were correctly formed `ddx-<8hex>`, it is not obvious that `ddx bead create` from inside an execute-bead worker is the right pattern at all. Two alternatives the design has not articulated a position on:

**(a) Append to the parent bead.** When the worker discovers sub-tasks, it appends them as structured items in the parent's description, notes, or a new `kind:discovered-subtask` event. The parent bead becomes the durable record of "what this run found"; future runs (or the operator) can decompose if/when the work warrants its own tracker entries.

**(b) Surface via the execute-bead result.** The agent emits a structured `discovered_subtasks: [...]` field on result.json; the loop / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.

**(c) Create new beads in-band (current behavior).** The worker calls `ddx bead create` directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue if the agent over-decomposes; complicates "what was THIS run's contribution" auditing.

Today's behavior is (c) but undocumented. The 11 read-coverage children landing as a single agent's decomposition is exactly the failure mode (c) makes easy: one analysis bead spawned 11 implementation beads in a single run, all P0, all without operator review. The work may be valid — but the pattern needs a position.

## In-scope outcomes

This bead's job is to surface both layers; the implementation work decomposes into follow-ups.

1. **Concrete fix for layer 1**: bead-id resolver should normalize worktree paths to the real project root before allocating an ID. Test: `ddx bead create` invoked with cwd=`<repo>/.execute-bead-wt-...` produces `ddx-<8hex>`, not `.execute-bead-wt-...-<random>`.
2. **Mass repair for the 12 existing malformed beads**: either rewrite their IDs to fresh `ddx-<8hex>` (with a redirect or alias map for any external references), or close them as deferred and re-file with proper IDs. Decision deferred until layer 2 is settled.
3. **Layer 2 decision**: file a separate design bead (or amendment to FEAT-006 / FEAT-010) that picks (a), (b), or (c) and documents the rationale. This bead does NOT pre-decide; it captures the question.

## In-scope files (layer 1 fix)

- `cli/cmd/bead_create.go` (or wherever bead-create resolves project context).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` or `cli/internal/bead/id.go` if ID generation lives there.
- A unit test that drops cwd into a worktree-shaped dir and asserts the resulting bead id matches `^ddx-[0-9a-f]{8}$`.

(Locate via: `grep -rn "execute-bead-wt\|FindProjectRoot\|projectRoot" cli/internal/bead/ cli/cmd/bead_*.go`.)

## Out of scope

- The 11 read-coverage child beads' actual implementation work — that's separate beads.
- Default behavior change for whether workers can/should create beads — that's the layer-2 design bead.
- GraphQL or MCP changes to handle long IDs — fix the source instead.

## Acceptance

Layer-1 only (this bead):

1. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/...` passes after the fix.
2. New unit test in `cli/internal/bead/` (or `cli/cmd/`) that simulates a bead-create call from `cwd=<project>/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-XXXXX-<timestamp>-<random>` and asserts the new bead's `id` field matches `^ddx-[0-9a-f]{8}$`. Test fails before the fix, passes after.
3. `grep -E '"id":"\.execute-bead-wt-' .ddx/beads.jsonl` returns either 0 (mass repair) or 12 with a recorded decision in this bead's notes that the existing 12 are deferred to a follow-up bead.
4. A separate bead is filed (or referenced existing one updated) that captures the layer-2 design question (a/b/c). Reference its ID in this bead's events.

## Why one bead, not an epic

Layer 1 is a tight 1-3 file fix. Layer 2 is a design discussion that should be its own artifact, not a sibling task. Splitting them keeps each bounded.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_model":"sonnet","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:02:48.370930131Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260429T215419-7600dda3","harness":"claude","model":"sonnet","input_tokens":35,"output_tokens":15621,"total_tokens":15656,"cost_usd":1.1730871000000003,"duration_ms":507667,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:02:48.465717142Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=15656 cost_usd=1.1731 model=sonnet"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"sonnet","resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:02:52.775820422Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"erik","body":"REQUEST_CHANGES

REACH-PROTO + REACH-GO have landed and ddx self-installs the check, run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current cli/ tree and resolve every violation. This is the systematic application phase the user mandated: "go back through the codebase systematically applying it."

ABSORBS
- ddx-09d2990c (WIRE) — the 4 specific unwired functions become a subset of this sweep. Close WIRE as superseded by this bead at file time.
- Any clusters from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md not already covered.
- Codex audit additions: jsonlFallbackForCollection (cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go:90), Store.breakStaleLock (lock.go:70), NewStoreWithBackend (store.go:119), WithSpokeHTTPClient (federation_spoke.go:103), Server.execStore (server.go:2028), GraphQL NewResolver (resolver.go:14).

EXPECTED WORKFLOW
1. Run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current worktree. Capture violation list.
2. For each violation, decide: WIRE (add to production code path) or DELETE (function is genuinely obsolete).
3. File one child bead per violation cluster (group by package or by originating story for traceability).
4. Drain the children. Each closes either via wiring PR or deletion PR.
5. Final pass: ddx ac run returns status=pass with no violations and no // wiring:pending annotations remaining (or all annotations cite still-open future beads).

DECISION RULE per violation
- If the symbol's originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE.
- If the symbol was added speculatively or the design has moved on → DELETE; reopen the originating bead with obsolescence rationale, or close as obsolete.
- If neither is clear within 15 min of investigation → annotate // wiring:pending <follow-up-bead> and file the follow-up.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744), REACH-GO (ddx-ea76e1b8)

NOT IN SCOPE

.ddx/beads.jsonl:609:{"acceptance":"1. `ddx bead reconcile --dry-run` or equivalent default dry-run command reports proposed lifecycle metadata repairs without mutating the tracker.
2. `ddx bead reconcile --apply` applies only supported store/API mutations and never edits .ddx/beads.jsonl directly.
3. TestBeadReconcile_LatestSuccessClearsStaleNoChangesMetadata covers a fixture where latest execute-bead event is success but Extra still says execute-loop-last-status=no_changes.
4. TestBeadReconcile_VerifiedNoChangesCanCloseAlreadySatisfied covers a verified no_changes rationale whose verification command passes.
5. TestBeadReconcile_NonRetryableNeedsInvestigationClearsCooldown covers a needs_investigation event with retry-after and preserves the triage label/reason.
6. TestBeadReconcile_ParentEpicMarksNotExecutableOnlyWithChildEvidence covers repeated parent/epic no_changes without creating new child beads.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestBeadReconcile_LatestSuccessClearsStaleNoChangesMetadata|TestBeadReconcile_VerifiedNoChangesCanCloseAlreadySatisfied|TestBeadReconcile_NonRetryableNeedsInvestigationClearsCooldown|TestBeadReconcile_ParentEpicMarksNotExecutableOnlyWithChildEvidence' -count=1 passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-05T20:44:22.956537264Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-837a2254","depends_on_id":"ddx-fd1766e5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-05T20:44:23Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-837a2254","depends_on_id":"ddx-49eeb476","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-05T20:44:23Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
The current tracker can contain stale management metadata from prior attempts: latest events may show success while execute-loop-last-status still says no_changes; verified no_changes may remain open; retry-after may be expired or attached to a non-retryable lifecycle condition; parent/epic beads may have repeated no_changes attempts even though children own the work. Without a reconciliation path, fixing future ddx work behavior strands the existing pile-up.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:744-752 SetExecutionCooldown writes mutable execute-loop-* Extra fields, but there is no companion repair path that reconciles those fields against append-only events.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:754-779 IncrNoChangesCount accumulates no_changes counters, but no command classifies or clears stale counters when a later terminal event supersedes them.
- cli/cmd/bead.go has cooldown show/clear around lines 1171-1273, but no broader lifecycle reconciliation dry-run/apply command.
- Live tracker evidence shows the failure class this bead must encode as fixtures: latest execute-bead event summary=success while execute-loop-last-status=no_changes; open verified no_changes; non-retryable needs_investigation parked with retry-after.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a supported reconciliation command or worker reconciliation pass, preferably `ddx bead reconcile`, with dry-run default and explicit apply mode.
- Reconciliation reads bead events and Extra fields through the store API, not by manual JSONL editing.
- Implement conservative rules only:
  - latest success/already_satisfied event may clear stale no_changes/cooldown Extra and close only when close evidence is present or verifiable through existing close path;
  - verified no_changes may be re-run/validated and closed as already_satisfied when the verification command passes;
  - expired retry-after may be cleared;
  - parent/epic repeated no_changes may be marked execution-eligible=false only when child/dependency evidence proves ordinary ddx work should not pick it;
  - non-retryable needs_investigation may clear retry-after and leave triage labels/blocked explanation.
- Output every proposed mutation with bead id, reason, current fields, proposed fields, and whether it is safe to apply.

NON-SCOPE
- Free-form human judgment or LLM review during reconciliation.
- Broad historical migration of closed beads.
- Manual edits to .ddx/beads.jsonl.
- Creating or deleting beads; this command only reconciles state on existing beads.

PARENT
- ddx-a9d130d0.

DEPS
- ddx-fd1766e5: reconciliation should use the same lifecycle action semantics as live execution.
- ddx-49eeb476: reconciliation output should use the same durable skip reasons exposed by queue diagnostics.

INTERSECTIONS
- Existing `ddx bead cooldown clear` handles one field manually; this bead should reuse or complement it without changing its simple behavior.
- Existing bead doctor repairs malformed/oversized tracker rows; this bead is lifecycle metadata reconciliation, not JSONL structural repair.","id":"ddx-837a2254","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:beads","area:cli","kind:feature","reliability","bead-lifecycle","spec:TD-031"],"parent":"ddx-a9d130d0","priority":1,"spec-id":"TD-031","status":"open","title":"beads: reconcile stale no_changes lifecycle metadata","updated_at":"2026-05-05T20:44:23.602318172Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:743:{"acceptance":"1. Root cause identified for why workers claim P2 beads while ready P0 beads exist (one of the 5 hypotheses above, or new finding). Investigation report at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/picker-priority-bug.md naming the exact code path that violates priority ordering.
2. Fix lands so that: with N ready beads sorted P0 < P1 < P2, a fresh worker (no prior claims) MUST claim a P0 first. Asserted by integration test TestExecuteLoop_ClaimsHighestPriorityFirst with a fixture queue of mixed priorities.
3. Concurrent-worker test: TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s — two workers running against a queue of 2×P0 + 4×P2 each claim a P0; only after both P0s are taken do they pick P2.
4. A diagnostic event is emitted when a worker passes over a higher-priority bead to claim a lower-priority one, with reason in {label_filter, in_attempted, claim_race, eligibility_filter, retry_cooldown}. So future starvation is observable.
5. Manual verification on this repo: the 4 P0 beads (ddx-dc157075, ddx-aee651ec, ddx-29058e2a, plus this bead) get claimed by the next freshly-started worker before any P2 bead.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"9cf40e4eb13ff019c60924b51f5eab50d9ce9c22","created_at":"2026-05-02T23:46:55.656187355Z","description":"OBSERVED
Two ddx workers (one server-managed, one --local --harness=claude) repeatedly claim P2 beads while four ready P0 beads sit unclaimed at the top of the queue.

REPRO (2026-05-02)
- ddx bead ready returns (top of list):
  - ddx-dc157075 P0
  - ddx-a946c744 P0 (triaged but child ddx-aee651ec P0 ready)
  - ddx-aee651ec P0
  - ddx-29058e2a P0
- Workers claim:
  - ddx-6d132f62 P2 (Story 16)
  - ddx-a7e7a1a1 P2 (Story 15-3)
  - ddx-297f840c P2 (Story 6 frontend) — earlier worker
- The 4 P0 beads have NO blocker:
  - extra.execute-loop-retry-after: null
  - extra.execution-eligible: null (defaults to true)
  - extra.superseded-by: null
  - all deps closed
- They appear in ddx bead ready AND should appear in Store.ReadyExecution() with priority=0 sorted first.

CODE PATH (per investigation)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1145 nextCandidate → calls Store.ReadyExecution()
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution → readyFiltered(true)
- store.go:1357 calls sortBeadsForQueue(ready) which sorts by priority asc (0 first; verified at store.go:1462, 1475)
- Loop returns first candidate not in attempted map (loop.go:1150-1158)

By design this MUST return P0 first. Yet workers are getting P2. Hypotheses:
1. The `attempted` map persists across what should be fresh worker runs (worker process lifecycle bug — possibly related to ddx-dc157075).
2. A label filter is silently set on the worker spec (the server-managed worker had empty harness/model — possibly empty label filter is treated as a regex that excludes some labels).
3. Two workers race on Claim() and the loser falls through, retrying nextCandidate which now sees the bead in attempted and skips to lower-priority.
4. The server-managed worker uses a different picker path than ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate (e.g., workers.go has its own selection that ignores priority).
5. The P0 beads have a label/area excluded by some default filter the operator doesn't see.

INVESTIGATION NEEDED
- Trace each currently-running worker: print its full spec (harness, model, labelFilter, oncce, pollInterval).
- Print the result of ReadyExecution() on demand (add a `ddx server workers next-candidate <worker-id>` debug command, or run from REPL).
- Add structured event when a worker passes over a P0 to claim a P2 — log the reason (in attempted, label-filtered, claim race, etc.).
- Inspect cli/internal/server/workers.go for any divergence from execute_bead_loop.go's nextCandidate.

LIKELY RELATED
